summary: Activated ADR 0124 on the live docker-runtime-lv3 NATS runtime by reconciling the PLATFORM_EVENTS JetStream stream to config/nats-streams.yaml, expanding the live jetstream-admin publish authorization to include platform.>, restarting lv3-nats-jetstream, and verifying a canonical platform.findings.observation publish reached the stream.

verification.observed: The first `make check-nats-streams` run reported that `PLATFORM_EVENTS` was absent. `make apply-nats-streams` created the stream from `config/nats-streams.yaml`, and the final `make check-nats-streams` run returned `PLATFORM_EVENTS: none`.

verification.check: Live NATS publish authorization for canonical platform topics
verification.observed: The live NATS auth file at `/opt/nats-jetstream/config/nats-server.conf` was backed up, updated so `jetstream-admin` can publish to `platform.>`, and `lv3-nats-jetstream` was restarted successfully on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

`uv run --with pyyaml --with nats-py python scripts/nats_streams.py --smoke-publish` returned an ack for `platform.findings.observation` on stream `PLATFORM_EVENTS` with sequence `1` after the auth update and container restart.

notes: The committed repo automation for the live stream contract landed in source commit `b1d67b3f214841da6d273bbf35258e2b5c629cf9` via `scripts/nats_streams.py`, `make check-nats-streams`, and `make apply-nats-streams`.

A manual server-side auth edit was unavoidable because the live NATS runtime config under `/opt/nats-jetstream/config/nats-server.conf` is not yet repo-managed. The on-host file was backed up to `/opt/nats-jetstream/config/nats-server.conf.bak.<timestamp>` before the change.
The broader ADR 0080 maintenance-window `$KV.maintenance-windows.>` publish-rights gap was not part of this live apply and remains separate from ADR 0124.
